title: NOC 65109 – Other sales related occupations | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 65101 (Other sales related occupations) is a versatile category that captures the essential support roles within Canada’s diverse retail and service landscape. This classification includes professionals such as rental agents (non-automotive), street vendors, and door-to-door distributors. As a TEER 5 occupation, it serves as a high-accessibility entry point into the Canadian economy. For immigration, these roles are frequently utilized in Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) and the Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP) to fill critical gaps in the service sector.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level5Major Group65 – Sales and service support occupationsSub-Major Group651 – Sales and service support occupationsMinor Group6510 – Sales and service support occupationsNOC Code65109

duties: Rent or lease various products such as tools, appliances, or sports equipment to customers.
Explain the operation and safety requirements of rented equipment.
Process payments and maintain records of rental agreements and inventory.
Sell or distribute products at street corners, at doorsteps, or in malls.
Demonstrate the features and benefits of products to potential customers at trade shows or retail locations.
Set up and maintain attractive displays to attract customers.
Collect payments and issue receipts for sold or distributed items.
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
On-the-job training is provided for all specific product lines and equipment operations.
Basic mathematical and record-keeping skills are necessary.
High proficiency in English or French (or both) is required for effective customer interaction.
A valid driver's license may be required for door-to-door distribution or equipment delivery.
job_outlook: The outlook for this occupation is Favourable. While some distribution roles are becoming digitized, the "Experience Economy" is driving a need for demonstrators and specialized rental agents who can provide human expertise and physical product handling. The natural movement of workers into supervisory roles ensures a continuous need for new international talent to fill these entry-level positions.
labour_market_demand: The demand for these specialized sales support roles is Stable. In 2026, we are seeing a significant rise in demand for Rental Agents (for equipment and tools) as the construction and DIY sectors expand across Ontario and British Columbia. Additionally, the "Gig Economy" has revitalized roles for independent distributors and street vendors in major urban tourist hubs like Montreal and Vancouver.
Low Entry Barriers: Start your Canadian career without needing extensive prior experience or high-level academic credentials.
Skill Diversification: Gain experience in inventory management, customer relations, and financial transactions.
Flexible Environments: Choose between indoor retail support, specialized rental agencies, or outdoor distribution roles.
PR Foundations: Accumulate the necessary Canadian work experience to qualify for semi-skilled immigration streams.
pathways: Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion pathway for workers with a job offer from a designated employer in the Atlantic provinces.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): A dedicated stream for those working in tourism, hospitality, or retail for at least 9 months in BC.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta on a valid work permit in eligible service categories.
Manitoba PNP (Skilled Worker in Manitoba): Targeting workers who have been employed in the province for at least 6 months.
